---
title: "Demo"
echo: false
---

## Overview

The diagram below shows how syncweaver coordinates a **source repo** and a **host repo**
through the CCBR/syncweaver orchestrator.

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    actor Dev as Developer
    participant Src as Source Repo<br/>(demo-syncweaver-source)
    participant SW as Orchestrator<br/>(CCBR/syncweaver)
    participant Host as Host Repo<br/>(demo-syncweaver-host)

    Note over Src: .github/workflows/<br/>syncweaver-source-dispatch.yml

    Dev->>Src: push release tag
    Src->>SW: repository_dispatch<br/>(source_repository, ref)

    SW->>Host: repository_dispatch<br/>(source_repository, ref)

    Note over Host: .github/workflows/<br/>syncweaver-host-update.yml

    Host->>Host: resolve source_path<br/>from .syncweaver-lock.json
    Host->>Src: fetch code at ref
    Host->>Host: vendor code into source_path
    Host->>Host: open pull request<br/>with updated vendored code

    Note over Host,Src: If the host has local patches...

    Dev->>Host: trigger contribute-patch
    Note over Host: .github/workflows/<br/>syncweaver-host-contribute-patch.yml
    Host->>Src: open pull request<br/>with patch applied upstream
```

## Repository layouts

This view emphasizes a different question than the sequence diagram: which
source repositories are vendored into which host capsules.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  subgraph H1[demo-syncweaver-host]
    H1A[code/hello]
    H1B[code/MOSuite]
  end

  subgraph H2[MOSuite-create]
    H2A[code/MOSuite]
  end

  subgraph H3[MOSuite-clean]
    H3A[code/MOSuite]
  end

  S1[demo-syncweaver-source<br/>modules/hello]
  S2[CCBR/MOSuite<br/>R package source]

  S1 --> H1A
  S2 --> H1B
  S2 --> H2A
  S2 --> H3A
```

In these examples, one host capsule can aggregate multiple upstream sources,
while other capsules vendor just a single shared source package.

### Source repo - monorepo

The source repo holds the canonical code. A single workflow template triggers
the dispatch chain on every release.

```
demo-syncweaver-source/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ syncweaver-source-dispatch.yml  в†ђ notifies orchestrator on release
в””в”Ђв”Ђ modules/
  в””в”Ђв”Ђ hello/                              в†ђ vendored into host as code/hello
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DESCRIPTION
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ NAMESPACE
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ R/
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ inst/
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ man/
```

### Host repo - Code Ocean capsule

The host repo consumes vendored code and carries a lockfile that pins each
dependency to a specific ref and git SHA.

```
demo-syncweaver-host/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ syncweaver-host-update.yml          в†ђ receives dispatch, opens update PR
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ syncweaver-host-contribute-patch.yml в†ђ sends local patches upstream
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .syncweaver-lock.json                        в†ђ pins source repos + refs
в””в”Ђв”Ђ code/
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ main.R
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ hello/           в†ђ vendored from demo-syncweaver-source modules/hello
  в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DESCRIPTION
  в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ NAMESPACE
  в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ R/
  в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ inst/
  в””в”Ђв”Ђ MOSuite/         в†ђ vendored from CCBR/MOSuite
```

### Source repo - MOSuite

This is a package-style source repository. The canonical implementation lives in
the package root, and the syncweaver source-dispatch workflow is defined under
`.github/workflows/`.

```
multiOmicsSuite/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ syncweaver-source-dispatch.yml  в†ђ notifies syncweaver on release
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DESCRIPTION
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ NAMESPACE
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ R/                                      в†ђ primary package implementation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ cli.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ clean.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ differential.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normalize.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ inst/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ extdata/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ quarto/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ man/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ tests/
```

### Host repo example - MOSuite-create capsule

This host repo is a Code Ocean capsule. It vendors source code into `code/`,
tracks upstream state in `.syncweaver-lock.json`, and keeps capsule runtime
files alongside the vendored package.

```
MOSuite-create/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ syncweaver-update-source.yml  в†ђ host-side sync workflow in this repo
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .syncweaver-lock.json                 в†ђ pins vendored sources to refs + SHAs
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ code/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ main.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ run/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ MOSuite/                          в†ђ vendored from multiOmicsSuite
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ environment/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Dockerfile
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ postInstall
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ metadata/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ metadata.yml
в””в”Ђв”Ђ tests/
```

## Repos

| Role | Example repo | Key file |
|------|-------------|----------|
| Source | [demo-syncweaver-source](https://github.com/NIDAP-Community/demo-syncweaver-source) | `.github/workflows/syncweaver-source-dispatch.yml` |
| Host | [demo-syncweaver-host](https://github.com/NIDAP-Community/demo-syncweaver-host) | `.syncweaver-lock.json`, `.github/workflows/syncweaver-host-update.yml` |
| Orchestrator | [CCBR/syncweaver](https://github.com/CCBR/syncweaver) | `.github/host-repositories.yml`, `.github/workflows/syncweaver-update-hosts` |

## Lockfile

The host repo tracks each vendored dependency in `.syncweaver-lock.json`:

```json
{
  "sources": {
    "code/hello": {
      "repo_url": "https://github.com/NIDAP-Community/demo-syncweaver-source",
      "ref": "main",
      "remote_subdir": "modules/hello"
    }
  }
}
```

When the source repo publishes a release, the dispatch chain updates the
vendored code at `code/hello` and opens a pull request in the host repo.
